untranslatable_reason: This signal describes an options calendar spread strategy with delta-adjusted position sizing. The core logic requires tracking: (1) delta values of two different options contracts, (2) dynamic quantity adjustments based on those deltas, (3) multi-leg option position management, and (4) tracking proximity of underlying to strike price at near-term expiration. None of these concepts can be expressed using only single-bar price/technical indicators. The backtest engine appears designed for equity/futures price-action rules, not options portfolio construction. Requires options pricing model (Black-Scholes or similar) and contract-level management.
translation_notes: Signal MCMILLAN_DRY_180 is fundamentally an options strategy requiring Greeks-based position management. A simplified equity proxy (e.g., 'buy when price near support in ranging market, exit on breakout') could approximate neutral stance but would lose the essential delta-hedging logic. Recommend implementing this as a separate options module rather than forcing into price-action rules engine.
